_meta.name: Shiboleth ground truth v1 (TurboTax corpus)
_meta.status: FROZEN. Approved as-is by Aarvin George on 2026-07-09 (chat approval; no corrections requested). This dataset is the MVP1 acceptance target and seeds the E3 checker golden set.
_meta.corpus: ground-truth/snapshots/ (54 pages, crawl4ai, hash convention: sha256 of whitespace-stripped body) + snapshots-synthetic/ (17 authored fixtures)
_meta.scorecard: doc 05 §1 VERBATIM 4 rules; library entry D-01

judgment_sources.analyst: individually judged by the compliance-analyst pass
judgment_sources.footer_inherited: the shared footer block judged once, inherited by every page containing it
judgment_sources.screened_policy: deterministic keyword screen; honest needs_review/not_applicable labels, never invented verdicts

records.evidence_quote: TurboTax Refund Advance is a loan based upon your anticipated refund and is not the refund itself. 0% APR and $0 loan fees.
records.location: shared footer, Refund Advance loan details and disclosures
records.reasoning: R-02 trigger: a rate of finance charge is stated (0%). Requirement: it must be stated as an APR. It is, literally: '0% APR'. Pass. No library entry governs APR language (axis B not applicable).

records.evidence_quote: File Now, Pay Later is available with a 3, 6, or 9 month loan term. Variable APR ranging from 15%-33%. For example, a 6-month $2,000 loan with an APR of 19% has a finance charge of $112.28 and 6 monthly installments of $352.05 each.
records.location: shared footer, File Now Pay Later loan details
records.reasoning: Trigger: finance-charge rates stated (15%-33%, example $112.28). Requirement: stated as APR. Explicitly satisfied, TILA-style, including a worked example. Exemplary pass; useful as a few-shot positive.

records.evidence_quote: TurboTax Free Edition ($0 Federal + $0 State + $0 To File) is available for those filing simple Form 1040 returns only (no forms or schedules except as needed to claim the Earned Income Tax Credit, Child Tax Credit, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A). More details are available here. Roughly 37% of taxpayers qualify.
records.location: shared footer, 'TurboTax Free Edition' offer footnote
records.reasoning: The footer's Free Edition footnote carries the substance of the approved disclosure (eligibility percentage + simple-1040 limitation) but the wording drifts from library entry D-01: 'Roughly 37% of taxpayers qualify' vs approved '~37% of filers qualify', and the schedule-exception phrasing is restructured ('no forms or schedules except as needed to claim...' vs 'no schedules, except for...'). Content-equivalent, wording-drifted: the canonical real-world example of compliant-but-drifted. Note: the footer footnote alone would NOT satisfy R-01's 'right underneath' placement for hero claims; placement is judged per page.

records.evidence_quote: Refer a Friend: Maximum of $500 in total rewards for 20 referrals.
records.location: shared footer, referral offer footnote
records.reasoning: R-04 (Reg DD-style) governs bonuses on ADVERTISED DEPOSIT PRODUCTS. The referral reward is compensation for referring tax-software customers, not a bonus for opening/maintaining a deposit account, and no deposit product is the advertised product in this footnote. Trigger not met; N/A, not a pass.

records.evidence_quote: Free Tax Filing with TurboTax Free Edition ... ~37% of filers qualify. Simple Form 1040 returns only (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A). $0 Federal + $0 State + $0 To File.
records.location: hero, immediately under the H1 free claim
records.reasoning: Trigger: multiple TurboTax Free / Free Edition mentions (H1, comparison table, features, closing CTA). Requirement: the approved disclosure must appear right underneath. It does, VERBATIM to D-01, at the hero, at the FreeTaxUSA comparison table, at the features header, and at the closing CTA. Placement and wording both satisfied. This page is the gold-standard pass for R-01 and the model for what compliant placement looks like.

records.evidence_quote: A simple Form 1040 return is one that's filed using IRS Form 1040 only (no schedules, except for EITC, CTC, student loan interest, and Schedule 1-A). Roughly 37% of taxpayers are eligible.
records.location: body FAQ, 'What qualifies as a simple tax return for TurboTax Free Edition?'
records.reasoning: Within the same page that passes R-01, the FAQ paragraph restates the disclosure with drifted wording: 'Roughly 37% of taxpayers are eligible' vs approved '~37% of filers qualify'. Substantively equivalent (compliant), textually divergent from D-01 (drift). A clean, real 'drifted but compliant' instance co-existing with verbatim usage elsewhere on the page: exactly the reconciliation finding type.

records.evidence_quote: You must (a) e-file your federal tax return with TurboTax and (b) currently have or open a Credit Karma Money(TM) Spend (checking) account with MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC.
records.location: shared footer on this page, Refund Advance eligibility terms
records.reasoning: Ambiguous trigger: the ADVERTISED product on P02 is tax software, not a deposit product; the checking account appears only inside loan eligibility fine print. Strict reading: R-03 not triggered (N/A). Cautious reading: any deposit-product mention in marketing must carry the $250,000 formulation, which this lacks. Recorded needs_review to preserve the ambiguity for the human gate; the unambiguous R-03 test lives on P18 where the deposit product IS the advertised product.

records.evidence_quote: (no bonus for an advertised deposit product appears on this page)
records.location: page-wide
records.reasoning: No deposit-account bonus stated anywhere on P02. Referral rewards in the footer are software referrals (see GT-F04). Trigger not met: N/A.

records.evidence_quote: Lock your debit card instantly, dispute transactions in-app, and get FDIC insurance up to $5M through a network of participating banks.
records.location: body, 'Protect your money' feature card
records.reasoning: Trigger clearly met: P18's advertised products are Credit Karma Money checking and savings accounts (deposit products: 'Free to open', 'high-yield savings', 'overdraft coverage'). Requirement: the FDIC language must state 'Deposit product is FDIC-insured up to $250,000 through [Bank]'. The page instead leads with 'FDIC insurance up to $5M through a network of participating banks', a sweep-network formulation that names no insuring bank at the claim and states a different amount than the required per-bank $250,000 formulation; footnote 2 says only 'Banking services provided by MVB Bank, Inc., Member FDIC' with no $250,000 statement for the Spend account. Under the scorecard as written this fails the required-language test. No approved library entry exists for FDIC language, so axis B is N/A-unapproved. Flagged as the page's primary violation; a compliance officer may accept the footnote-9 mechanics as adequate under general FDIC signage rules, but the scorecard's specific formulation is not met, and that is what we check.

records.evidence_quote: Credit Builder plan requires you to open a line of credit and a Credit Builder savings account, both banking services provided by Cross River Bank, Member FDIC. Credit Builder savings account is a deposit product, insured up to $250,000.
records.location: footnote 5, Credit Builder
records.reasoning: For the Credit Builder savings account specifically, the footnote states it is a deposit product insured up to $250,000, with the providing bank (Cross River Bank, Member FDIC) named in the same footnote. This satisfies R-03's required elements for that product. Pass, and a useful contrast to GT-P18-01 on the same page: same rule, two products, opposite outcomes.

records.evidence_quote: Free to open. No monthly fees. No minimum balance.
records.location: body, account feature cards
records.reasoning: P18 advertises deposit-account features (free to open, no fees, early paycheck, overdraft coverage) but states no BONUS (no premium/gift for opening or maintaining the account). Reg DD-style bonus disclosures are not triggered by fee-free features. The high-yield savings rate claim ('rate higher than the national average') is an APY-adjacent claim but no bonus is stated and no specific rate is quoted in the extracted text; noted for the record. Trigger not met: N/A.

records.evidence_quote: (TurboTax Free Edition customers are entitled to payment of $30.)
records.location: shared footer, Maximum Refund Guarantee footnote
records.reasoning: Strict-reading residue: 'TurboTax Free Edition' is mentioned inside guarantee/legal footnotes on ~44 pages without the eligibility disclosure right underneath. Strict reading of R-01 ('If Turbotax free is mentioned...') makes every such mention a violation; practical reading exempts non-promotional legal references. Recorded once at footer scope as needs_review for the human gate to set policy; the checker endpoint should reproduce this ambiguity class rather than silently pick a side.

records.evidence_quote: $0-$139 State additional for paid products. Start for free. Pay only when you file.
records.location: online hub, Do It Yourself pricing card
records.reasoning: Trigger (analyst interpretation): the $0 bottom of the DIY price range IS TurboTax Free Edition, so the card advertises the free product. Requirement: no eligibility disclosure right underneath; the 37% language exists only in the shared footer footnote far from the claim. Placement violation under R-01 as written. Counter-reading acknowledged: '$0-$139' is a range price and 'Start for free / Pay only when you file' is the separate pay-when-you-file offer (footnoted in the footer); a reviewer could dismiss on that basis. Confidence reflects the interpretive step; this is precisely the kind of flag the disposition gate exists to confirm or dismiss.

records.location: comparison page, Do It Yourself pricing card
records.reasoning: Same pattern and reasoning as GT-P05-01: free-tier price advertised on the comparison page without adjacent eligibility disclosure; 37% language footer-only. Same counter-reading, same confidence.

Strong demo example of reconciliation catching claims the scorecard didn't anticipate.
